data.title: Curve Proposes Recovery Mechanism for Bad Debt in CRV-long Market
data.description:
Curve's team has introduced a proposal on their governance forum to address approximately $700,000 in bad debt from the CRV-long LlamaLend market. According to ChainCatcher, the proposed recovery mechanism leverages the option characteristics of CRV-long treasury assets. The value of these assets increases with a rise in CRV prices, while a price drop does not further diminish their value.

The proposal suggests creating a Curve stable pool with a low amplification factor (A=2) and a high exchange fee (1%), concentrating the liquidity of treasury tokens around a 71% solvency level. Arbitrageurs can profit by purchasing treasury tokens through flash loans and partially liquidating them.

Curve DAO is invited to approve incentives for this pool, with management fees collected in the form of treasury tokens retained in the fund. If successful, this mechanism could serve as a reference solution for similar situations in the future.

data.title: Japan's Central Bank Faces Dilemma Amid Middle East Crisis, Analyst Says
data.description: On April 27, Vishnu Varathan from Mizuho Securities commented that the oil price shock caused by the Middle East conflict could disrupt demand and exacerbate existing inflation risks. According to Jin10, the Bank of Japan is expected to maintain its current policy stance this week. The central bank is in a difficult position, as it is unclear which risk will dominate and what measures should be taken in response. The high inflation and significant depreciation pressure on the yen, which existed before the Middle East crisis, limit the Bank of Japan's ability to preemptively ease policy to counter potential growth shocks. At the same time, the bank cannot raise interest rates too quickly or significantly, as this could undermine the prospects for achieving sustainable and healthy reflation.

data.title: Four Pillars Secures Series A Funding to Connect Asian and Global Markets
data.description: Four Pillars has successfully secured Series A funding from Pantera Capital and Further Ventures, achieving a valuation of 30 billion won. According to NS3.AI, the company aims to connect Asian and global markets, bridging traditional finance with Web3 technologies. Additionally, Four Pillars plans to expand its focus from research into providing solutions.

data.title: Kbank Partners with Ripple to Test Blockchain Remittance Technology
data.description: Kbank has announced a partnership with Ripple to explore blockchain-based overseas remittance technology. According to NS3.AI, the collaboration aims to leverage Ripple's global payment network and infrastructure to evaluate improvements in processing speed, cost efficiency, and transaction transparency. This initiative reflects Kbank's commitment to enhancing its financial services through innovative technology.

data.title: An account that had lost over $3.4 million purchased $250,000 worth of NBA Western Conference First Round Game 4, in which the Rockets defeated the Lakers.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, in Polymarket's prediction event of "NBA Western Conference First Round Game 4 Rockets vs. Lakers," an account that lost over $3.4 million (0x492442eab586f242b53bda933fd5de859c8a3782-1766317541188) purchased approximately $250,000 worth of shares predicting the Rockets would defeat the Lakers. The average opening price was 59¢, and the current price is 59.5¢, resulting in a floating profit of approximately $2,117. The Lakers currently lead the Rockets 3-0 in the NBA Western Conference First Round playoffs, with Game 4 scheduled for today. The Lakers' starting lineup is Ayton, Kennard, James, Hachimura, and Smart; the Rockets' starting lineup is Amen, Smith Jr., Sheppard, Ethan, and Shen Jing. Both teams are affected by injuries, and there is some market speculation regarding whether the Rockets will avoid a sweep. Odaily Seer continuously focuses on the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Prices Decline as Crude Oil Rises
data.description: Gold prices experienced a decline of 0.69%, settling at $4,677.81 per ounce, according to NS3.AI. Meanwhile, both WTI and Brent crude oil saw an increase of approximately 1.1% on the same day. Additionally, the BTC Volatility Index and ETH Volatility Index showed slight increases, reaching 41.80 and 62.19, respectively.

data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong Optical Communications Sector Sees Early Gains
data.description: Hong Kong's optical communications sector experienced a strong start, with several companies showing notable increases in stock prices. According to Jin10, Yangtze Optical Fibre and Cable (06869.HK) saw a rise of over 7%, while Inno-Tech Holdings (01729.HK) increased by more than 4%. Other companies such as Hua Hong Semiconductor (01347.HK), Joyson Electronics (00699.HK), and Cambridge Industries Group (06166.HK) also followed suit with gains.

data.title: Polymarket to Upgrade V2 Exchange on April 28
data.description: Polymarket, a prediction market platform, is set to upgrade its V2 exchange on April 28 at 19:00 UTC+8. According to Foresight News, the platform will undergo a downtime of approximately one hour during the upgrade. The V1 order book will be cleared, and all pending limit orders will be canceled. Users trading via API are required to update to the latest SDK before the upgrade, as the V1 client will no longer be operational post-upgrade.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Open Mixed as Tech Shares Rise
data.description: Hong Kong's stock market opened with mixed results today. According to Jin10, the Hang Seng Index experienced a slight decline at the opening, while the Hang Seng Tech Index rose by 0.56%. Early trading saw strength in sectors such as gold, non-ferrous metals, and artificial intelligence concept stocks. Meanwhile, ZTE Corporation (00763.HK) shares fell by 1.73% following its earnings report.

data.title: Solana Network Integrates AAVE via Sunrise Protocol
data.description: Solana announced that AAVE has been launched on the Solana network through the DA layer interoperability protocol, Sunrise. According to Foresight News, this integration aims to enhance the network's capabilities and expand its ecosystem.

data.title: Large SOL Stake Withdrawn and Transferred to CEX
data.description: On April 27, a significant movement of SOL tokens was observed. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, a major SOL stakeholder withdrew 300,000 SOL from staking and subsequently transferred them to a centralized exchange (CEX). This activity occurred within the past half-hour, highlighting notable shifts in SOL holdings.

data.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman Outlines Five Operational Principles
data.description:
OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has outlined five operational principles for the company. According to Odaily, Altman emphasized that AI's critical decisions should be driven by democratic processes rather than solely by laboratories. He defended OpenAI's increased investment in computing power, the construction of data centers, and vertical integration strategies.

Altman also mentioned the potential challenges posed by strong AI models, such as lowering the threshold for biological risks. He reiterated OpenAI's commitment to an iterative deployment strategy, noting that there might be times when user access to model capabilities could be restricted for safety reasons, aiming to balance empowerment with system resilience.

data.title: Altcoin Season Index at 42, Below Threshold for Altcoin Season
data.description: CoinMarketCap's Altcoin Season Index currently stands at 42. According to NS3.AI, this index evaluates the 90-day performance of the top 100 coins by market capitalization, excluding stablecoins and wrapped coins, in comparison to Bitcoin. CoinMarketCap defines an altcoin season as a period when 75% of these coins outperform Bitcoin. The current index value indicates that the market is not in an altcoin season.

data.title: Grayscale Highlights Preferred Stocks as High-Volatility Bitcoin Bets
data.description: Grayscale has identified preferred stocks issued by digital asset companies, such as Strategy's STRC, as resembling CCC-rated Bitcoin investments with an implied annual volatility of 50%. According to NS3.AI, Grayscale emphasized that spot Bitcoin ETPs continue to be the optimal choice for direct Bitcoin exposure in brokerage or retirement accounts.

data.title: US media: Iran proposes a deal to reopen the Straits of Hormuz, but postpones nuclear negotiations.
data.description: According to AXIOS, citing a US official and two sources familiar with the matter, Iran has made a new proposal to the US, suggesting prioritizing an agreement on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and lifting the maritime blockade, while postponing nuclear negotiations to a later stage. This new proposal aims to break the current deadlock in negotiations and bypass disagreements within the Iranian leadership regarding the scope of nuclear concessions they are willing to make to reach an agreement with the Trump administration. According to three US officials, President Trump is expected to hold a situation room meeting on Iran with his senior national security and foreign policy team on Monday. One official stated that the meeting is expected to discuss the current impasse in negotiations with Iran and possible next steps in a potential war. (Jin Shi)

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenClaw and Hermes Agent Compete in AI Agent Growth
data.description: OpenClaw and Hermes Agent are two of the fastest-growing AI agent projects on GitHub. According to PANews, these projects are being compared in terms of design philosophy, cost efficiency, and differences in real-world workflows. This analysis aims to assist users in selecting the most suitable personal AI agent.

data.title: Ethereum Surpasses 2400 USDT Mark
data.description: Ethereum has seen a notable increase in its value. According to Odaily, the cryptocurrency has surpassed the 2400 USDT threshold, currently trading at 2400.15 USDT. This marks a 24-hour increase of 3.68%.

data.title: Bitcoin Surpasses 79,000 USDT Mark
data.description: Bitcoin has surpassed the 79,000 USDT mark, according to Odaily. The current price is reported at 79,014.4 USDT, reflecting a 24-hour increase of 1.94%.

data.title: Whale Address Faces Significant Losses on BTC and ETH Short Positions
data.description:
PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter). According to analyst Yu Jin, the whale address pension-usdt.eth has incurred substantial unrealized losses after shorting Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) with a threefold leverage at the beginning of the month. The address initially shorted approximately $110 million worth of BTC and ETH at the market's low point. However, as the market rebounded in April, these positions have resulted in an unrealized loss of about $15.25 million.

Specifically, the address shorted 1,000 BTC at an average price of $67,992, leading to an unrealized loss of approximately $10.57 million, with a liquidation price around $99,394. Additionally, it shorted 20,000 ETH at an average price of $2,132, resulting in an unrealized loss of about $4.68 million, with a liquidation price near $3,400.

data.title: A whale shorted $110 million worth of BTC and ETH with 3x leverage, and is currently experiencing a paper loss of $15.25 million.
data.description: According to crypto analyst EmberCN, the whale pension-usdt.eth shorted approximately $110 million worth of BTC and ETH at the beginning of the month with 3x leverage, and is currently experiencing a floating loss of approximately $15.25 million. Specifically, the address opened a short position of 1,000 BTC at $67,992, currently showing a floating loss of approximately $10.57 million, with a liquidation price of $99,394; and opened a short position of 20,000 ETH at $2,132, currently showing a floating loss of approximately $4.68 million, with a liquidation price of $3,400.
